decks.notes: This UW Control deck focuses on using counterspells, removal, and card draw to control the game and outvalue the opponent. It aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while building up card advantage and eventually winning through a combination of powerful threats like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria and Elspeth, Storm Slayer. The companion Kaheera, the Orphanguard provides additional value and synergy with the deck's creatures. The deck also includes some land destruction elements with Field of Ruin to disrupt the opponent's mana base.

This Selesnya Aggro deck focuses on quickly deploying creatures and disrupting opponents with cards like Archon of Emeria and Thalia, Guardian of Thraben. Collected Company helps dig for more threats while Skyclave Apparition handles problematic permanents. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of efficient creatures and disruptive spells, using cards like Ouroboroid and Enduring Innocence to outvalue opponents. The mana base is designed to support the aggressive game plan, with a mix of dual lands and mana dorks to ensure smooth curves and consistent pressure.

This aggro deck focuses on utilizing cheap spells and creatures to quickly deal damage to the opponent. With cards like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage, the deck aims to create a fast and aggressive start, while also having the ability to control the board with cards like Reckless Rage and Burst Lightning. The deck's mana base is optimized for consistency, allowing for easy access to all the necessary colors for its spells. The overall goal is to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of quick creatures and burn spells.

The main focus of this UR Aggro deck is to quickly deploy low-cost creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage, while using burn spells like Reckless Rage and Monstrous Rage to control the board and clear a path for damage. Experimental Synthesizer and Cori-Steel Cutter provide additional threats and versatility. The deck aims to maintain pressure on the opponent and close out games quickly, utilizing cards like Stormchaser's Talent and Stock Up for added reach and card advantage. The mana base is optimized for consistent casting of spells and creatures on curve.

The key concept of this deck revolves around using Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to create a powerful combo that can overwhelm your opponent. By generating tokens with Greasefang and using Parhelion II to create more tokens and deal damage, you can quickly gain control of the game. Additionally, cards like Bitter Triumph, Cache Grab, and Witherbloom Command provide support and synergy to enhance the combo even further. The deck aims to efficiently execute the combo while maintaining board control and disrupting your opponent's strategy.

The main concept of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive strategy using creatures like Archon of Emeria, Skyclave Apparition, and Thalia, Guardian of Thraben. The deck also utilizes mana acceleration with cards like Llanowar Elves and Elvish Mystic to cast powerful spells like Collected Company and Michelangelo's Technique. The inclusion of cards like Ouroboroid and Toski, Bearer of Secrets provide card advantage and resilience against removal. The mana base is focused on green and white sources to support the aggressive game plan of the deck.

This UR Aggro deck focuses on quickly establishing a strong board presence and dealing damage to the opponent efficiently. With a mix of low-cost aggressive creatures like Soul-Scar Mage and Monastery Swiftspear, along with burn spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage, the deck aims to put pressure on the opponent early and maintain tempo throughout the game. Utilizing cards like Stormchaser's Talent and Riverpyre Verge enhances the deck's ability to draw cards and control the board, while also providing additional reach to close out games.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly play aggressive creatures like Cori-Steel Cutter, Emberheart Challenger, and Soul-Scar Mage, supported by burn spells like Fiery Impulse and Reckless Rage to remove blockers and push for damage. The deck aims to maintain a fast pace and overwhelm the opponent with a combination of creatures and direct damage spells, while using cards like Academic Dispute and Boomerang Basics for disruption and tempo advantage. The mana base is optimized for fast plays and consistent casting of spells.

The deck focuses on creating a powerful board presence and overwhelming the opponent with a combination of creatures and spells. By utilizing cards like Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II, the player can quickly build a threatening board state while disrupting the opponent's strategy with cards like Thoughtseize and Cache Grab. The deck also includes powerful removal options like Bitter Triumph and Witherbloom Command to deal with opposing threats. Overall, the key concept of the deck is to quickly assemble a winning combination of cards to secure victory in Pioneer 1 vs 1 matches.

This deck aims to quickly assemble the Greasefang-Parhelion combo to overwhelm opponents with a powerful offensive force. It uses cards like Bitter Triumph, Cache Grab, and Formidable Speaker to search for pieces of the combo, while also including protective creatures like Iron-Shield Elf and Overlord of the Balemurk. The deck also includes disruption in the form of Thoughtseize and Witherbloom Command to control the opponent's game plan. By efficiently executing the combo and supporting it with control elements, this deck can dominate the battlefield and secure victory.

This deck focuses on using Niv-Mizzet Reborn as a powerful control tool to gain card advantage and control the board. It utilizes a diverse range of multicolored spells to maximize Niv-Mizzet's ability to draw cards, with additional cards such as Up the Beanstalk for ramp and Leyline Binding for removal. The deck also includes companions like Yorion, Sky Nomad for additional synergy and flexibility. By leveraging a mix of lands and versatile spells, this deck aims to outvalue and outmaneuver opponents in a Pioneer 1 vs 1 format.

The main concept of this UR Aggro deck is to aggressively control the battlefield by using burn spells and efficient creatures to quickly deal damage to the opponent. The deck utilizes cards like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage to apply pressure early on, while also using spells like Torch the Tower and Burst Lightning to remove threats and clear the way for attacks. The deck aims to close out the game quickly through aggressive play and efficient use of damage-dealing spells.

The deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan through a combination of removal spells, discard effects, and resilient threats. It aims to control the board with creatures like Sentinel of the Nameless City and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse while using cards like Fatal Push and Thoughtseize to disrupt the opponent's strategy. The deck also utilizes cards like Mutavault and Blooming Marsh for mana acceleration and to fix its mana base. Overall, the deck seeks to outvalue the opponent and grind out a win through attrition and resource management.

The deck focuses on controlling the opponent's actions through counterspells, removal, and card draw. It uses cards like Dovin's Veto, Teferi, Hero of Dominaria, and Supreme Verdict to maintain board control and prevent the opponent from building a strong presence. The inclusion of Kaheera as a companion adds an extra layer of versatility to the deck. By utilizing a combination of card advantage and disruptive spells, the deck aims to outvalue and outmaneuver the opponent in order to secure victory.

This Dimir Control deck focuses on using disruption spells like Thoughtseize and fatal push to control the board and disrupt the opponent's game plan. It also utilizes bounce effects like Boomerang Basics and Stormchaser's Talent to keep the opponent's threats in check. The deck relies on Yorion, Sky Nomad as its companion, allowing for additional value with its blink ability. With a variety of lands to provide mana fixing and utility, this deck aims to outvalue and outlast the opponent while maintaining control of the game.

The main idea of this deck is to establish early aggression with fast and efficient creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage, while using cards like Reckless Rage and Fiery Impulse to control the board and remove opponent's threats. The deck also includes card draw and spell recursion to maintain pressure and momentum. The mana base is optimized for casting spells quickly and efficiently to maintain tempo and keep the pressure on the opponent. Overall, this deck aims to win quickly through aggressive plays and efficient spell usage.
